$comment: Task #631 — Curated public preset library. Each preset bundles a sensitivity calibration plus per-engine weights so PSIRT/triage teams can pick a starting profile from /presets and deep-link to /check?preset=<id>. Weights are informational descriptors of how each scoring engine is emphasised; the /check page applies sensitivity + slop thresholds to local settings when a preset is loaded. Keep weights in [0, 2] and sensitivity in {lenient, balanced, strict}. Edit this file (and bump the version when shipping changes) — there is no admin UI for v1.

presets.name: PSIRT Aggressive
presets.description: Tuned for PSIRT inboxes drowning in AI-generated noise. Strict sensitivity with heavy weighting on factual fabrication and template detection so obvious slop fails fast.
presets.audience: PSIRT teams triaging public disclosure mailboxes

presets.name: Bounty Triage Conservative
presets.description: Balanced calibration for paid-bounty triage. Avoids penalising terse human-authored reports while still catching wholesale fabrication.
presets.audience: Bug bounty triagers who pay per valid report

presets.name: Maintainer Minimal Noise
presets.description: Lenient profile for solo open-source maintainers. Surfaces only high-confidence slop so first-time human contributors are not scared off.

presets.name: AppSec Internal Pentest
presets.description: For internal AppSec teams reviewing reports from contracted pentesters. Boosts factual & template signals, expects high evidence quality.

presets.name: Academic Disclosure
presets.description: For academic researchers writing long-form coordinated disclosures. Linguistic patterns common in papers (formal voice, hedging) are de-emphasised.

presets.name: AI-Assisted Allowlist
presets.description: Acknowledges that human reporters increasingly use LLMs to draft. Down-weights linguistic AI signals while keeping factual + template scrutiny intact.
presets.audience: Programs that explicitly allow LLM-assisted authoring

presets.name: Vendor Coordinator
presets.description: For vendor coordinators ingesting reports from many programs. Balanced weights, slightly stricter than default to flag obvious cross-program duplicates and templates.

presets.name: Research Strict
presets.description: Maximum sensitivity for calibration / corpus research where false negatives are worse than false positives.
